Web search results for query: "CSS columns masonry CSS-only card grid no JavaScript 2025 variable height cards"

Links: [{"title":"Masonry layout - CSS - MDN Web Docs","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"title":"39 Creative CSS Masonry Layouts","url":"https://wpdean.com/css-masonry/"},{"title":"Build a Masonry Layout 🧱 with CSS Columns (No JS) - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_1Bhd6Oatto"},{"title":"CSS Only Masonry Layouts with Grid","url":"https://fjolt.com/article/css-grid-masonry"},{"title":"10+ CSS Masonry Layouts: Free Code Snippets & Examples","url":"https://freefrontend.com/css-masonry-layouts/"},{"title":"Masonry layout - CSS | MDN","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"title":"Build a Masonry Layout with Pure CSS column-width","url":"https://theosoti.com/short/masonry-layout/"},{"title":"Masonry with CSS Grid, finally a solution without JavaScript | utilitybend","url":"https://utilitybend.com/blog/masonry-with-css-grid-finally-a-solution-without-javascript"},{"title":"https://griffa.dev/posts/building-a-responsive-progressively-enhanced-masonry-layout-with-only-css-and-html/ | Medium","url":"https://griffadev.medium.com/building-a-responsive-progressively-enhanced-masonry-layout-with-only-css-and-html-998b2de89cf2"}]

I've found comprehensive information about CSS-only masonry layouts for card grids with variable height. Here's a summary of the current approaches:

## Modern CSS Masonry Methods (2025)

### 1. **CSS Grid with Masonry (Native but Limited Browser Support)**

The most common approach uses `display: grid-lanes` along with `grid-template-columns`, where items lay out according to the masonry algorithm—each row of items loads into the column with the most room, causing a tightly packed layout without strict row tracks.

Example syntax:
```css
.grid {
  display: grid-lanes;
  gap: 10px;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr));
}
```

In browsers that do not support masonry, regular grid auto-placement will be used instead.

### 2. **CSS Multi-Column Layout (Best Cross-Browser Support)**

The lightest implementation uses `column-count` and `column-gap` properties, with items ordering vertically within each column, top to bottom, then moving to the next column.

Key benefits:
- Items automatically pack into columns without any JavaScript calculation.
- No JavaScript required, works across all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

For variable-height cards:
Apply `break-inside: avoid` to card elements to prevent items from splitting across column boundaries, keeping CSS card hover effects and content intact.

### 3. **Performance Advantages**

JavaScript-based masonry can delay content rendering, affecting Core Web Vitals, while CSS-only methods render faster and score better on Lighthouse audits.

### Important Accessibility Note

Always test reading flow and item ordering, because visual columns can differ from the underlying DOM sequence users navigate, and visual placement should not conflict with logical DOM sequence.

The **CSS columns** approach is the most reliable for immediate production use with variable-height cards, while **CSS Grid masonry** offers more layout control for browsers that support it.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
